codeReview.summary: The fix correctly updates the Borrow Call Flow, Contract Dependency Graph, Dual-VM Execution Boundary, and the PVM-unavailable failure-mode wording, but it does not fully resolve the original architecture-diagram blocker because the retained System Overview Mermaid diagram still presents the legacy pre-M6 quote-ticket/PvmQuoteProbe framing.

issues.description: `README.md:45` still labels RiskAdapter as `quote ticket publication`, and `README.md:52` still labels the PVM engine as `PvmQuoteProbe` inside the top-level System Overview Mermaid diagram. The fix commit `57293bb` only updated the Borrow Call Flow, Contract Dependency Graph, Dual-VM Execution Boundary, and failure-mode row, so the README still contains a retained architecture diagram that documents the legacy pre-M6 design instead of the post-M6 unified risk gateway with inline deterministic math as the canonical path and PVM as optional verification. The original blocking honesty issue therefore remains unresolved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The docs-worker skill still lacks an explicit checklist item to re-audit every retained Mermaid diagram and architecture label in README after architecture-sensitive doc fixes, not just the specific sections named in the fix request.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The fix worker updated three targeted diagrams and the failure-mode table, but the unchanged System Overview Mermaid block in README.md:31-53 still uses `RiskAdapter (quote ticket publication)` and `PvmQuoteProbe`, reproducing the same architecture-drift pattern already identified in round 1.

summary: Review failed. The targeted diagrams and failure-mode wording were improved, but the README System Overview diagram still documents the legacy quote-ticket/PvmQuoteProbe architecture, so the original docs-diagram honesty blocker is not fully fixed.
